features.name: Plates Application
features.description: The top-level Datagrok app and tree-browser that lets users navigate, open, and explore assay plate collections stored on the server.
features.evidence_summary: func:Plates:Plates (role:app), platesAppTreeBrowser (role:appTreeBrowser), and Assay Plates all share the application-level namespace and together constitute the entry-point user experience.

features.name: Plate File Import
features.description: Detects and ingests assay plate files in TXT, XLSX, and CSV formats, routing each to the appropriate parser and storing the result in the platform.
features.evidence_summary: All five functions carry fileHandler / format-detection roles for TXT, XLSX, and CSV, forming a complete import pipeline.

features.name: Plate Visualization
features.description: Renders assay plates as interactive grid views within file previews, folder browsers, and inline grid cells, giving users an at-a-glance layout of well data.
features.evidence_summary: previewPlate, viewPlateXlsx, platesFolderPreview (all fileViewer/folderViewer roles), and PlateGridCellRenderer (cellRenderer) share the sole purpose of visually rendering plate data in the UI.

features.name: Plate Retrieval & Barcode Lookup
features.description: Fetches individual plates or plate lists from the backing database, including barcode-based lookup and record counting.
features.evidence_summary: Function and query both named getPlateByBarcode, plus getPlates/getPlatesCount, collectively implement the plate-level lookup surface.

features.name: Well Data Access
features.description: Retrieves per-well measurement values, roles, and property metadata, enabling downstream analysis and display of individual well readings.
features.evidence_summary: All five queries operate on the well level (values, roles, properties) and share the `plts` connection, forming a coherent well-data retrieval API.

features.name: Plate Property Management
features.description: Defines, enumerates, and filters the custom properties attached to plates, supporting dynamic metadata schemas and allowed-value constraints.
features.evidence_summary: These queries all operate on the plate-property schema layer (names, allowed values, creation), sharing the `plts` connection and a clear property-management naming pattern.

features.name: Plate Templates
features.description: Creates and manages plate templates that pre-define the layout, plate-level, and well-level properties for new assay plates.
features.evidence_summary: All ten queries share `Template` in their names and manage the lifecycle of plate templates and their associated plate/well property definitions.

features.name: Analysis Runs
features.description: Records, parameterises, and queries analysis runs executed against plate data, enabling reproducible bioassay result tracking.
features.evidence_summary: createAnalysisRun, saveAnalysisRunParameter, saveAnalysisResult, and the query/group retrieval queries all share the `AnalysisRun`/`Analysis` name root and form the complete analysis-run CRUD surface.
